Stand in front of a box or bench with your feet shoulder-width apart with a kettlebell in a goblet position.
Lower your body into a squat position, sitting back onto the box and pausing for a moment before standing back up.

Alternate legs, keeping your chest up and core engaged.

Take a big step backwards with your left leg, lowering your body until your left thigh is parallel to the ground and your right knee is bent at a 90-degree angle.

Step forward with one leg, lowering until both knees form 90° angles.

Grab a stick of some sort or hold onto something stable (rack, bench).
Grab a box that is knee height or slightly lower.
Hold the stick in your right arm, kettlebell in the other and put your right leg on the box.
Begin your squat by breaking at the knees. Get your knee close to the floor or all the way to the floor. Make sure to keep your chest tall.

description: Stand on a platform, kettlebells in front rack position, feet hip-width apart.

description: Step back, lowering knee toward floor, keeping chest upright and core engaged.

description: Push through front foot, return to start.

Step back, lowering knee toward floor, keeping chest upright and core engaged.
Push through front foot, return to start.

Stand perpendicular to a wall, using one arm against the wall for balance. With your other arm, grab the top of your foot.
Pull your leg upwards and back to engage your quads, pausing at the apex of the stretch for a few seconds.
Return to starting position and repeat with your other leg.

Pause at the apex of the stretch, and slowly return to starting position. Repeat with your other leg.

Keeping your upper legs flat on the ground, pull both of your feet towards your buttocks.
Pause at the apex of the stretch, then return to starting position.
Lay flat on your belly. Extend one arm out in front of you, placing your palm on the floor. With your other arm, grab the top of your foot.
Keeping your upper leg flat on the floor, pull your foot towards your buttocks to engage the stretch.
Pause for a few seconds, and then repeat the stretch with your other leg.

Get your elbows directly underneath the bar, squat straight up and then walk back a few steps.
Break at your knees and hips to initiate the squat. Make sure you maintain a flat back.

workout_name: Band Seated Cervical Side Flexion Hold
description: Wrap a ribbon/flat band around your head and hold the ends to the side of your head with one arm.

workout_name: Band Seated Cervical Side Flexion Hold
description: Extend the elbow slightly till you feel the opposite side of your neck resisting the band

workout_name: Band Seated Cervical Side Flexion Eccentric
description: Sit on a bench maintaining an upright posture.

workout_name: Band Seated Cervical Side Flexion Eccentric
description: Wrap a ribbon/flat band around your head and hold the ends to the side of your head with one arm.

workout_name: Band Seated Cervical Side Flexion Eccentric
description: Gently extend the arm till the band starts applying force onto the neck muscles then bend your arm so that the band resistance is reduced but not eliminated.
Sit on a bench maintaining an upright posture
Wrap a ribbon/flat band around your head and hold the ends to the side of your head with one arm
Gently extend the arm till the band starts applying force onto the neck muscles.
Lie on your back with your knees bent and your feet on the ground to allow for a flat lower back.
Elongate your neck as much as possible
Tuck the chin in and press the back of your head into the ground
It helps having a folded towel under the head.
Pull your shoulders back and away from the ears and straighten the back
Circumduct the head both clockwise and anti-clockwise making sure you are working on the end range throughout the movement.
Pull your shoulders back and away from the ears and straighten the back.
Lean your head back trying to keep the front of the neck straight.
Point your chin to the ceiling.
Pull your shoulders back and away from the ears and straighten the back.
Tuck your chin in and drop your head forwards without rounding the back.
Pull your shoulders back and away from the ears and straighten the back.
Rotate your head from side to side without rounding your shoulders.
Pull your shoulders back and away from the ears and straighten the back.
Lean your head to the side (bring your ear to your shoulder) without shrugging your shoulder or leaning the spine to the side.
Place a foam roller on the floor perpendicular to your body.
Sit and lie back onto it aiming for your mid back to be in contact with the roller.
Gently tilt your hips backward, squeeze glutes and engage core to protect the lower back.
Support head on palms, elbows rotated in. Slightly lift hips off the ground.
Stand up straight and keep your shoulders relaxed.
Bring your shoulder blades in towards the spine.
Rotate palms out to help squeeze the muscles in between the shoulder blades.

workout_name: Laying Levator Scapulae Mobilisation Massage Ball
description: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor. Keep your arm by your side

workout_name: Laying Levator Scapulae Mobilisation Massage Ball
description: Place a massage ball under the area between the top of your scapula and spine with the other arm.

workout_name: Laying Levator Scapulae Mobilisation Massage Ball
description: Once you find the tight/sore spot, move your arm over your head and back to starting position to mobilise your levator scapula muscle.
Lie on your front, arms outstretched to the side.
Raise head and chest off the ground.
Raise and lower your arms (flapping wings motion) by contracting and relaxing the muscles in between your shoulder blades.
From the standing or seated position straighten your back.
Pull the shoulders back but keep them relaxed.
Move shoulders away from your ears and return to the starting, relaxed position.
From the standing or seated position straighten your back.
Pull the shoulders back but keep them relaxed.
Move shoulders forward and return to the starting, relaxed position.
From the standing or seated position straighten your back.
Pull the shoulders back but keep them relaxed.
Move shoulders backwards and return to the starting, relaxed position.
